unit.name: Latent diffusion and diffusion transformers for text-to-image
unit.description: Shows how practical image generation uses latent diffusion, VAE/GAN autoencoders, and diffusion transformers.
unit.summary: Latent diffusion is introduced as the dominant practical form of diffusion: first train an encoder-decoder to map high-resolution images into a smaller latent space, often downsampling spatially so the diffusion model operates on a cheaper representation [ts=3154s], [ts=3162s]. The diffusion model is then trained on noisy latents rather than raw pixels, with the encoder frozen while latents are extracted [ts=3195s], [ts=3222s]. At inference time, the model denoises a random latent repeatedly, then the decoder converts the clean latent back into an image [ts=3232s]. The encoder-decoder is typically a VAE with a very small KL prior weight, but VAE decoders can be blurry, so modern LDM pipelines add a GAN-style discriminator to improve perceptual sharpness [ts=3266s], [ts=3304s]. This is why modern pipelines combine VAE, GAN, and diffusion components [ts=3335s]. The lecture then introduces diffusion transformers (DiTs), standard Transformer-block diffusion models whose key architectural issue is how to inject timestep and conditioning information [ts=3357s], [ts=3373s]. Timestep conditioning is commonly inserted via predicted scale/shift, while text or other conditioning is inserted through cross-attention or joint attention [ts=3397s], [ts=3424s]. Text-to-image systems pass a prompt through a frozen text encoder such as T5 or CLIP, combine text embeddings with noisy latents in a diffusion transformer, and decode clean latents into an image [ts=3469s], [ts=3493s].

key_points.text: Latent diffusion first trains an encoder-decoder that maps images to a lower-dimensional latent space.
The diffusion model is trained on noisy latents, with the encoder frozen during diffusion training.
At inference, a random latent is denoised repeatedly and decoded into an image.
The latent encoder-decoder is typically trained like a VAE with a small KL prior weight.
A GAN-style discriminator is added because plain VAE decoders tend to produce blurry outputs.
Modern latent diffusion pipelines combine VAE, GAN, and diffusion components.
Diffusion transformers use standard Transformer blocks; the core architectural question is conditioning injection.
Text-to-image latent diffusion uses frozen text encoders such as T5 or CLIP to condition a diffusion transformer.

kp_catalog.name: Latent diffusion models
kp_catalog.description: LDMs run diffusion in a compressed latent space rather than pixel space to make high-resolution generation tractable.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because latent diffusion models formalizes how lDMs run diffusion in a compressed latent space rather than pixel space to make high-resolution generation tractable, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: VAE-GAN-diffusion pipeline
kp_catalog.description: Modern LDM systems combine a VAE-like autoencoder, GAN-style discriminator, and diffusion model.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because vAE-GAN-diffusion pipeline synthesizes modern LDM systems combine a VAE-like autoencoder, GAN-style discriminator, and diffusion model into an end-to-end method or integrated recipe.

kp_catalog.name: Diffusion transformer conditioning
kp_catalog.description: DiTs use Transformer blocks and inject timestep and text/image conditioning via scale-shift, cross-attention, or joint attention.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because diffusion transformer conditioning clarifies diTs use Transformer blocks and inject timestep and text/image conditioning via scale-shift, cross-attention, or joint attention, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Text-to-image diffusion pipeline
kp_catalog.description: Text prompts are embedded by frozen language/text encoders and used to condition latent diffusion transformers.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because text-to-image diffusion pipeline clarifies text prompts are embedded by frozen language/text encoders and used to condition latent diffusion transformers, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
